commit.message:
feat(redesign): premium visual overhaul, number reconciliation, performance fix

Visual:
- Hero carousel: SVG chevron arrows (w-12 h-12), gradient CTA buttons
- Typography: eliminated all sub-10px text, minimum now text-xs (12px)
- Sub-pages: unique color-identity hero banners (cyan/mercado, green/tech, purple/pesquisa, red/seguranca)
- Panorama: larger KPIs, larger section headers, premium glass styling

Numbers reconciled:
- Countries: 972→37 (filter ≥20 jobs, exclude garbage)
- Insights: 104→46 (only publishable)
- Sources: 85→36 (real count)
- Removed fake 'Capital Rastreado US$ 1.4T' KPI (funding data is mis-sourced)
- Replaced with 'Tech Trends' KPI using real tech_trends data (38K signals)
- Fixed '~85% EUA' to '~54% EUA'
- Patents filtered: 37K mock-generated excluded

Methodology page:
- Replaced fake source list (CrunchBase, PitchBook, LinkedIn API) with real connected sources
- Added REAL/MOCK badges per KPI (10 real, 10 mock)
- Added honesty disclaimer about unconnected sources

Performance:
- Created materialized view mv_papers_vs_jobs (85s query→<100ms)
- Charts API now returns in <1s instead of timing out

Panorama charts:
- Replaced empty 'Investimento por Tipo' panel with 'Geopolítica ACLED' (929K events)

Fix Dependabot security alerts #45, #47, #48: upgrade npm and Python build dependencies (#1065)

* Initial plan

* Upgrade mocha and add overrides to fix npm security vulnerabilities

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/d7dbd434-2d27-4a08-914d-d8410e3a63e4

* Address Dependabot alerts: upgrade setuptools and actions/checkout

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/fe063606-4785-4a11-ba16-6dcbdaaa7c80

* Drop Python 3.8 support: incompatible with setuptools>=78.1.1

setuptools 78.x requires Python >= 3.9, so building for Python 3.8
fails after the setuptools minimum was raised to 78.1.1. Remove 3.8
from the CI test matrix and all release scripts (Linux, macOS, Windows),
and update the twine upload environments from py3.8 to py3.9.

---------

commit.message:
refactor(server): replace string-based block categorization with flags/tags (R5.4)

Replace 8 hardcoded string-matching functions in placement.rs with O(1)
BlockStateFlags checks and O(log n) BlockTags lookups:

- is_replaceable_block → BlockStateId::is_replaceable() flag
- is_interactable_block → BlockStateId::is_interactable() flag + beds tag
- is_wall_mountable → oxidized:wall_mountable tag
- is_player_direction_block → oxidized:player_direction tag
- is_sign_block → minecraft:all_signs tag
- is_door_block → minecraft:doors tag
- is_bed_block → minecraft:beds tag
- is_tall_plant → oxidized:tall_plants tag

Also:
- Add AIR_ITEM_NAME constant in item_stack.rs
- Add get_block_state_id() to ServerHandle trait
- Fix cmd_setblock keep-mode to use BlockStateId::is_air() (handles
  cave_air and void_air)
- Add 56 regression tests
- Update phase document

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix circuit-sim for Windows: text rawfile + encoding

- parse_rawfile.py: Support both binary and text (Values:) rawfile
  formats. Windows ngspice outputs text format even with -r flag.
- run_sim.py: Add encoding='utf-8' to all NamedTemporaryFile and
  Path.read_text() calls. Fixes UnicodeEncodeError on Windows
  where the default encoding is cp1252.

Tested: all three example netlists (rc_lowpass, pulse_response,
rc_sweep) pass end-to-end on Windows with ngspice 45.2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Windows/PowerShell cross-platform support

- Add install.ps1 for circuit-sim, elmer-fem, netlist-to-schematic
  (modeled after memory's existing install.ps1)
- Add top-level install.ps1 that iterates skills/*/install.ps1
- Add .gitattributes enforcing LF for .sh, CRLF for .ps1
- Update all READMEs with Windows PowerShell installation instructions
- Fix elmer-fem SKILL.md: replace sed/inline env var with cross-platform
- Fix memory SKILL.md: remove bash-specific mkdir -p and date syntax
- Fix AGENTS.md /tmp/ paths with relative paths (cross-platform)
- Add explicit encoding='utf-8' to run_sim.py Path.read_text()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rewrite README for a warmer first impression

- Lead with bold value prop instead of dry definition
- Add 'Who is this for?' section so readers self-identify
- Reframe skills table as benefits ('What it does for you')
- Move Quick Start below the pitch (why before how)
- Fold Item Registry and Templates into What's Inside table
- Consistent conversational tone throughout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(world): implement block tag loading from vanilla data (R5.3)

Add compile-time block tag registry generated by build.rs from vanilla
tags.json and custom Oxidized tag files. Tags are stored as static
sorted arrays enabling O(log n) membership testing with zero startup
cost.

Vanilla tags: 248 block tags loaded from tags.json (produced by
bundle_tags.py), containing block type IDs for categories like
minecraft:doors, minecraft:beds, minecraft:replaceable, etc.

Custom Oxidized tags (4):
- oxidized:interactable (33 blocks) — crafting tables, furnaces, etc.
- oxidized:wall_mountable (64 blocks) — buttons, wall torches, etc.
- oxidized:player_direction (130 blocks) — beds, doors, stairs, etc.
- oxidized:tall_plants (8 blocks) — sunflower, tall_grass, etc.

Public API: BlockTags (zero-sized) with contains(), get(), tag_count(),
tag_names(). TagSet wraps sorted slice with O(log n) contains().

17 unit tests + 3 doc tests verify vanilla/custom tag membership,
positive/negative checks, sort invariants, and unknown tag handling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(world): Enrich BlockStateEntry with physics properties (R5.2)

Add 10 new fields to BlockStateEntry: light_emission, light_opacity, hardness,
explosion_resistance, friction, speed_factor, jump_factor, map_color, and
push_reaction. All values are extracted at compile-time from vanilla data and
stored in fixed-point format for O(1) access.

BlockStateEntry grows from 4 bytes to 18 bytes (525 KB total for 29,873 states).

Changes:
- Expand BlockStateEntry struct with 10 new properties (repr(C) layout)
- Update build.rs to load all properties from block_properties.json.gz
- Implement 9 accessor methods on BlockStateId with proper conversions:
  - light_emission/light_opacity: direct u8 access
  - hardness/explosion_resistance: f64 from fixed-point ×100
  - friction/speed_factor/jump_factor: f64 from fixed-point ×10000
  - map_color/push_reaction: direct u8 access
- Fixed-point encoding handles extreme values via clamping (e.g. barrier)
- All 186 unit tests pass; spot-checked 8 well-known blocks

This eliminates the need for string-based physics property lookups and
hardcoded arrays in oxidized-game/src/physics/block_properties.rs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(world): enrich BlockStateFlags with data-driven block properties (R5.1)

Expand BlockStateFlags from u8 (3 flags) to u16 (12 flags) per ADR-012.
New flags: IS_SOLID, HAS_COLLISION, IS_OPAQUE, IS_REPLACEABLE,
HAS_BLOCK_ENTITY, TICKS_RANDOMLY, REQUIRES_TOOL, IS_FLAMMABLE,
IS_INTERACTABLE.

Add Python extraction script (tools/extract_block_properties.py) that
parses Blocks.java builder chains, BlockEntityType.java, and class
hierarchy to produce per-block property data for all 1168 vanilla blocks.

Update build.rs to read block_properties.json.gz and compute flags.
Add 9 convenience methods on BlockStateId for O(1) flag checks.
Add 10 verification tests covering flag correctness.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix: print styles were hiding last/4th card bottom cut line

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add bottom cut line to last stargram card

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(phases): add Phase R5 — Data-Driven Dispatch & Code Deduplication

Plan 12 sub-tasks addressing four categories of tech debt found in
post-R4 audit:

- R5.1–R5.5: Enrich BlockStateFlags (u8→u16, 3→13 flags), expand
  BlockStateEntry with block properties, implement block tag loading,
  replace 114+ string-based block categorizations with flags/tags,
  replace hardcoded physics overrides with registry data
- R5.6: Replace hardcoded 5-biome match with registry lookup
- R5.7–R5.9: Extract packet codec helpers & roundtrip test macro,
  command registration helpers, standardize decoder error handling
- R5.10–R5.12: Decompose oversized structs (45-field ServerPlayer etc.),
  break down long functions (>200 LOC), replace magic numbers with
  named constants

Implements ADR-011 (tags) and ADR-012 (enriched block state) which were
accepted but had a large implementation gap. Data pipeline follows the
established pattern: extraction script → committed .json.gz → build.rs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Clean typography: single sans-serif font, solid divider, allergies always shown

- Single font (system-ui sans-serif) throughout
- 3 sizes only: 0.75rem labels, 0.85rem message, 0.9rem values
- Weight used for hierarchy: 700 labels, 600 names, 400 values
- Solid vertical divider between left/star, inset from top/bottom
- Allergies field always visible (shows dash if empty)
- Dashed cut lines between cards, no outer border

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Polish stargram cards: dotted cut lines, cleaner typography, bigger star

- Dashed cut lines between cards (no outer border)
- Serif labels (Georgia) for a polished print feel
- Inline label: value layout with natural wrapping
- Allergies only shown when relevant (not 'no'/'none')
- Message in italic serif below To/From with subtle separator
- Vertically centered content in all columns
- Larger star (150px)
- Clean B&W print output, 4 per page

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(server): fix 6 block interaction and world persistence bugs

1. Seed persistence: save level.dat immediately after new-world
   initialization so seed and spawn_y survive crashes before the
   first autosave tick.

2. Bed head placement: read the facing property from the foot block
   state instead of recalculating with an incorrect .opposite() call.
   The head is now placed in the correct direction (matching vanilla
   BedBlock.setPlacedBy).

3. Block facing reversed: add is_player_direction_block() to identify
   blocks that face in the player look direction (beds, doors, stairs,
   fence gates, trapdoors, repeaters, comparators). Remove the incorrect
   piston/observer special case that omitted .opposite() — vanilla
   DirectionalBlock uses .getOpposite() for those too.

4. Player self-placement: add AABB intersection check that prevents
   placing blocks inside the player bounding box (0.6×1.8 standing,
   0.6×1.5 sneaking), preventing self-suffocation.

5. Block use vs placement priority: check if the clicked block is
   interactable (crafting table, chest, furnace, bed, etc.) and the
   player is not sneaking before attempting placement. When the block
   is interactable and the player is not sneaking, placement is
   suppressed (vanilla: state.useItemOn() takes precedence).

6. Spawn Y / void fall: same root fix as #1 — immediate level.dat
   save ensures spawn_y=-60 is persisted for flat worlds instead of
   defaulting to the legacy spawn_y=64 on reload.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(server): persist world seed and fix biome deserialization

- ServerHandle::seed() now returns the actual world seed from level_data
  instead of hardcoded 0
- New worlds initialize world_seed from config: parse as i64 for numeric
  seeds, hash string seeds, or generate random if empty
- Biome deserialization now looks up actual registry IDs from biome names
  in the alphabetically-sorted vanilla biome list instead of using
  sequential indices (0,1,2...), which caused plains (ID 40) to become
  badlands (ID 0) after save/load — producing wrong skybox and colors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix 5 misclassified task_type values in item registry

govern was used as a catch-all for newer items. Reclassified:
- VariableLibrary: govern -> develop (legend already listed it as develop)
- DataAgent: govern -> distribute (exposes data via conversational AI)
- GraphModel: govern -> prepare (models data into graph structures)
- GraphQuerySet: govern -> prepare (saved graph queries, like KQLQueryset)
- OperationsAgent: govern -> track (monitors operations, like Reflex)

Only Ontology correctly remains as govern (metadata governance).
Updated \ legend descriptions to match.

fix(server): chunk persistence, block states, and double-block placement

Wire chunk load/save into the play loop and tick cycle:
- Add AsyncChunkLoader + ChunkSerializer to ServerContext
- get_or_create_chunk() checks DashMap → disk → generate
- Autosave dirty chunks alongside level.dat on tick interval
- Flush dirty chunks on graceful shutdown
- Remove chunks from dirty set only after successful write

Apply block state properties during placement:
- Calculate facing from player yaw (opposite direction)
- Determine axis from clicked face for logs/pillars
- Set half/type from cursor Y position for slabs/trapdoors
- Wall-mountable blocks use clicked face direction

Implement double-block placement and breaking:
- Doors: place lower + upper half at y+1
- Beds: place foot + head in facing direction
- Tall plants: place lower + upper half at y+1
- Breaking one half removes the companion block
- Companion placement checks for replaceable space

BREAKING CHANGE: ServerContext now requires chunk_loader and
chunk_serializer fields.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Exclude gitignored files from Lambda layer and source code bundles (#7)

* Initial plan

* feat: exclude gitignored files from lambda layer and code bundles

- Add gitignore_exclude_patterns() utility to bundler.py that reads the
  root .gitignore and returns all non-comment, non-blank patterns
- DepsBundler.__init__ now stores patterns; try_bundle passes them to
  shutil.copytree(ignore=...) so __pycache__, *.pyc, .venv/, etc. are
  excluded when copying workspace packages into the layer
- hello_stack.py passes exclude=gitignore_exclude_patterns() with
  ignore_mode=IgnoreMode.GIT to Code.from_asset for the lambda handler,
  keeping gitignored artefacts out of the deployment zip
- Add 5 new tests covering the new function and copytree ignore behaviour

Co-authored-by: klemjul <60812459+klemjul@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/klemjul/python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate/sessions/bbd1571f-8fa5-4142-b027-9555f94766b0

* fix: apply ignore callback at top level of workspace src copy

Replace the manual entry-by-entry loop with a single shutil.copytree call
on src_dir directly, so the ignore callback (built from .gitignore patterns)
is applied at every level including the top level of src/. The old loop
only passed ignore= when recursing into sub-directories, meaning top-level
__pycache__/, *.pyc, and similar entries would still be copied into the layer.

Co-authored-by: klemjul <60812459+klemjul@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/klemjul/python-aws-monorepo-boilerplate/sessions/d44fe4c5-8e1b-43e0-9182-b623837951e5

* revert: undo gitignore exclusion changes from bundler, hello_stack, and tests

Convert test files from Fixie to xUnit framework

- Add [Fact] attributes to all public test methods
- Add using Xunit and Xunit.Abstractions to all test files
- Add ITestOutputHelper constructors to all TestBase-derived classes
- Replace TextWriter Console with ITestOutputHelper Output in TestBase
- Add OutputWriter convenience property for RecordingLogger bridging
- Create TestOutputHelperWriter adapter (TextWriter wrapping ITestOutputHelper)
- Update QueueConfigurationExtensions to accept ITestOutputHelper
- Update all Console references to Output/OutputWriter as appropriate
